original_abstract:
In recent years, the study of artificial intelligence (AI) has undergone a profound paradigm shift, driven by the revolutionary capabilities of generative models in both supervised and unsupervised learning contexts. Generative AI (GenAI) has achieved state-of-the-art performance in tackling complex real-world challenges across diverse sectors, including image-to-image translation, medical diagnostics, natural language processing, and multimodal data synthesis. 

This paper provides a systematic review and analysis of recent technical advancements in the field, with a focus on application-specific models. While the most visible impacts of GenAI have occurred in language generation through Large Language Models (LLMs) and advanced image synthesis, this review offers a coherent synthesis of contemporary breakthroughs across various interdisciplinary domains. Beyond cataloging current techniques, the paper explores the future trajectory of the field, outlining emerging trends and potential research avenues. Finally, the discussion concludes with an examination of Responsible AI principles, highlighting the essential ethical considerations and sustainability frameworks required to guide the continued growth of these powerful generative systems.
humanized_abstract:
Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) is rapidly emerging as the new paradigm in the field of Artificial Intelligence (AI). In the last few years generative models achieved spectacular success for a wide variety of applications ranging from supervised such as image-to-image translation, and self-driving cars, to unsupervised learning such as disease diagnosis in medical images and ultra high speed real time language translation of several human languages for multimodal data.

This paper undertakes a systematic review and analysis of recent developments, focusing on application specific models. Although generative capabilities of GenAI have been widely demonstrated in the domain of language with Large Language Models (LLMs) and image synthesis, this paper aims to provide a synthesis of contemporary advances in other interdisciplinary fields of research. In addition to reviewing the state of the art of current technology in GenAI, the paper provides a perspective on future developments and possible research directions. Finally, it then concludes with a discussion of Responsible AI, which highlights the need for compliance with ethical and sustainability guidelines.
